rationale: Ground truth has no substantive concerns (just a bot notice and an 'lgtm' approval), so both candidates correctly approve this trivial mechanical rename with full recall. Precision favors Y: its cited file/line numbers for every changed site align almost exactly with the actual diff hunks, while X cites test_serving_speech.py:2638,2651,2720 for the three test edits, which are off by roughly 100 lines from where those changes actually occur (2533/2546/2615), suggesting a fabricated detail. Y also adds a near-miss substring sweep for extra rigor, though its output is cluttered with irrelevant run-report metadata that X's cleaner format avoids.
